perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees and undertakes that it shall not, without first obtaining the written consent of the other, disclose or make available to any person, reproduce or transmit in arty manner, or use (directly or indirectly) for its own benefit or the benefit of others, any Confidential Information save and except both parties may disclose any Confidential Information to their Affiliates, directors, officers, employees or advisors of their own or of Affiliates on a “need to know” basis to enable them to evaluate such Confidential Information in connection with the negotiation of the possible business relationship; provided that such persons have been informed of, and agree to be bound by obligations which are at least as strict as the recipient’s obligations hereunder. For the purpose of this Agreement, Affiliates shall mean, with respect to any party, any other person directly or indirectly Controlling, Controlled by, or under direct or indirect common Control with, such party. “Control”, “Controlled” or “Controlling” shall mean, with respect to any person, any circumstance in which such person is controlled by another person by virtue of the latter person controlling the composition of the Board of Directors or owning the largest or controlling percentage of the voting securities of such person or by way of contractual relationship or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees and undertakes that it shall not, without first obtaining the written consent of the other, disclose or make available to any person, reproduce or transmit in arty manner, or use (directly or indirectly) for its own benefit or the benefit of others, any Confidential Information save and except both parties are encouraged to disclose any Confidential Information to their Affiliates, directors, officers, employees or advisors of their own or of Affiliates on a “need to know” basis to enable them to evaluate such Confidential Information in connection with the negotiation of the possible business relationship; provided that such persons have been informed of, and agree to not be bound by obligations which are at least as strict as the recipient’s obligations hereunder. For the purpose of this Agreement, Affiliates shall mean, with respect to any party, any other person directly or indirectly Controlling, Controlled by, or under direct or indirect common Control with, such party. “Control”, “Controlled” or “Controlling” shall mean, with respect to any person, any circumstance in which such person is controlled by another person by virtue of the latter person controlling the composition of the Board of Directors or owning the largest or controlling percentage of the voting securities of such person or by way of contractual relationship or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'may disclose' to 'are encouraged to disclose' and 'agree to be bound by obligations which are at least as strict as the recipient's obligations hereunder' to 'agree to not be bound by obligations which are at least as strict as the recipient's obligations hereunder' in section 3, this directly contradicts section 4, which mandates the receiving party to protect Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall use the same degree of care and protection to protect the Confidential Information received by it from the disclosing party as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a like nature, and in no event such degree of care and protection shall be of less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the obligation to protect Confidential Information. The original text states parties 'may disclose' to affiliates under the condition that those affiliates 'agree to be bound by obligations which are at least as strict as the recipient’s obligations hereunder'. The changed text alters this to 'are encouraged to disclose' and changes the condition to affiliates 'agree to not be bound by obligations which are at least as strict as the recipient's obligations hereunder'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The receiving party shall use the same degree of care and protection...'. The changed text implies that affiliates who receive the information are not obligated to protect it at all, while the contradicted text places a clear obligation on the receiving party to protect the information.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall use the same degree of care and protection to protect the Confidential Information received by it from the disclosing party as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a like nature, and in no event such degree of care and protection shall be of less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall use only minimal effort to protect the Confidential Information received by it from the disclosing party as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a like nature, and in no event such degree of care and protection shall be of less than a basic degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the same degree of care and protection' to 'only minimal effort' in Section 4 introduces contradiction with Section 8, which states that both parties acknowledge that Confidential Information has implications regarding future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information coming to the knowledge of the other may relate to and/or have implications regarding the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage. Accordingly neither party shall use the Confidential Information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes, information, and/or competitive and strategic advantage of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'the same degree of care and protection' with 'only minimal effort' for protecting confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes that confidential information has significant implications for future strategies and competitive advantage, and that neither party should use the information in a manner that would jeopardize or adversely affect the disclosing party's interests. Minimal effort is not compatible with protecting future strategies and competitive advantages, establishing a clear and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to indemnify and keep indemnified each other against all loss and damage, which the disclosing party may suffer as a result of any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; provided always that the disclosing party shall forthwith give written notice to the receiving party of the above loss and damage and satisfactory documentary evidence of such actual loss and damage.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to indemnify and keep indemnified each other against all loss and damage, which the disclosing party may suffer as a result of any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; provided always that the disclosing party shall forthwith give written notice to the receiving party of the above loss and damage; however, the receiving party is not responsible for any damages, as the information is exchanged with no guarantees.
perturbation.explanation: Adding a clause that the 'receiving party is not responsible for any damages' to the indemnity clause in section 6, makes the initial agreement to 'indemnify and keep indemnified each other against all loss and damage' contradictory. This contradicts the entire purpose of indemnity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree to indemnify and keep indemnified each other against all loss and damage, which the disclosing party may suffer as a result of any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; provided always that the disclosing party shall forthwith give written notice to the receiving party of the above loss and damage; however, the receiving party is not responsible for any damages, as the information is exchanged with no guarantees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'however, the receiving party is not responsible for any damages, as the information is exchanged with no guarantees' to the original indemnity clause. This directly contradicts the initial agreement to 'indemnify and keep indemnified each other against all loss and damage,' negating the purpose of the indemnity and creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, making the contradiction immediately apparent.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain valid for a period of three (3) years from the date of execution of this Agreement which term may be extended by mutual consent in writing of both the parties. This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party without assigning any reason whatsoever. The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement. In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain valid indefinitely from the date of execution of this Agreement. This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party without assigning any reason whatsoever. The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement. In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the validity period from 'three (3) years' to 'indefinitely', contradicting the subsequent clause stating 'The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding...for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement' in section 14.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain valid indefinitely from the date of execution of this Agreement. This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party without assigning any reason whatsoever. The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement. In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 3-year validity period, after which obligations extended for 3 more years. The changed text makes the agreement valid 'indefinitely'. This directly contradicts the clause that limits obligations to 'a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement', as there is no longer a defined expiry. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, where this contradiction takes place. This is a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction as one part states 'indefinitely' and another part states 'three years after termination/expiry'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings with respect to the Confidential Information and constitutes the entire Agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. If any term or provision of this Agreement is determined to be illegal, unenforceable, or invalid in whole or in part for any reason, such illegal, unenforceable, or invalid provisions or part(s) thereof shall be stricken from this Agreement and such provision shall not affect the legality, enforceability, or validity of the remainder of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings with respect to the Confidential Information and constitutes the entire Agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. Furthermore, this agreement does not supersede any other agreement, written or verbal, between the parties. If any term or provision of this Agreement is determined to be illegal, unenforceable, or invalid in whole or in part for any reason, such illegal, unenforceable, or invalid provisions or part(s) thereof shall be stricken from this Agreement and such provision shall not affect the legality, enforceability, or validity of the remainder of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'Furthermore, this agreement does not supersede any other agreement, written or verbal, between the parties', this contradicts the prior statement that 'This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings with respect to the Confidential Information and constitutes the entire Agreement' in section 17.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings with respect to the Confidential Information and constitutes the entire Agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. Furthermore, this agreement does not supersede any other agreement, written or verbal, between the parties. If any term or provision of this Agreement is determined to be illegal, unenforceable, or invalid in whole or in part for any reason, such illegal, unenforceable, or invalid provisions or part(s) thereof shall be stricken from this Agreement and such provision shall not affect the legality, enforceability, or validity of the remainder of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that 'This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings' establishing it as the sole agreement. The changed text adds 'Furthermore, this agreement does not supersede any other agreement, written or verbal, between the parties,' which directly contradicts the previous statement. The contradicted text, which is a restatement of the changed text, therefore also contains this same contradiction within itself. This creates a clear in-text contradiction and makes the contract unclear regarding which agreements are binding.
